channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: LEGO Batman: Legacy of the Dark Updated PC System Requirements Revealed
item.content:encoded:
TT Games has revealed the updated system requirements for running LEGO Batman: Legacy of the Dark Knight on PC.

Players had received a list of PC specs for LEGO Batman: Legacy of the Dark back in February 2026; however, that was from a Steam listing, and this is directly from the developers. What’s more, the new set of system requirements is more forgiving than the previous list; check it out below.

LEGO Batman: Legacy of the Dark PC System Requirements Downgraded

According to a new post by the official LEGODCGame account on X (formerly Twitter), we now have the PC system requirements for LEGO Batman: Legacy of the Dark Knight. The minimum and recommended CPU and GPU requirements have been reduced; however, the 16 GB of RAM and 50 GB of SSD storage requirements remain the same for minimum and recommended. What’s more, players now have a new set of requirements to run the game on 4K.

Check out the updated minimum, recommended, and 4K PC system requirements for LEGO Batman: Legacy of the Dark in the table below:

In other news, LEGO Batman: Legacy of the Dark Knight features over 100 suits and other collectibles. Additionally, the upcoming game’s open world is larger than Arkham Knight’s. What are your thoughts on LEGO Batman: Legacy of the Dark Knight PC system requirements? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Gang Beasts seemingly coming to PS5. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

item.title: Gang Beasts PS5 Port Appears To Be in Development
item.content:encoded:
It seems that Gang Beasts could be the next game to be ported to the PS5.

Bringing classic popular titles to current-generation consoles is always a wise decision. It allows former players to indulge in nostalgia while introducing new players to these games on modern platforms. Recently, we witnessed the re-release of Red Dead Redemption, and it seems that Gang Beasts is next in line.

Gang Beasts PS5 Listing Found on ESRB Website

Spotted on r/GamingLeaksAndRumours, a new listing has appeared on the ESRB website, pointing towards Gang Beasts’ PS5 Port. There’s not much information shared in the listing other than the mention of ‘PlayStation 5’ under the platforms section. The description for the game states, “Gang Beasts is rated E10+ for Everyone 10+ by the ESRB with Cartoon Violence.” There is no official announcement from the developers yet, though if a rating has appeared, a release could be close.

What is Gang Beasts

Gang Beasts is a multiplayer party game developed by Boneloaf and released in 2017. In the title, players get to play as gelatinous characters who must engage in various fight sequences across different hazardous environments, which almost always turn into hilarious situations. It features local and online multiplayer, where you can fight enemies in the Melee game mode or against each other in various stages set in Beef City through the Gang mode.

In other news, new rumors and claims are surfacing that the PlayStation 6 could debut with immediate backward compatibility for all PS4 and PS5 games. Additionally, Housemarque has revealed all the PlayStation 5 and PS5 Pro features for Saros, including updated PSSR. What are your thoughts on Gang Beasts coming to PS5? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about PAC-MAN Snack Breaks releasing next month. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

item.title: Battlefield 6’s Vehicles Facing Major Changes in Season 3
item.content:encoded:
Battlefield 6’s next major update drops on May 12, and Electronic Arts’ teams are busying themselves shaping up what’s coming next. In an all-new blog post on Steam and social media, Chris Matte, one of DICE’s many game designers, explained what’s happening with vehicles in season three.

There’s quite a lot changing. From how vehicles take damage to how they handle and perform under intense incoming fire, players will find that season three alters their experiences across the board, whether they’re rocking four wheels or a set of tracks.

Battlefield 6 Vehicle Update Coming Soon

In February, DICE revealed that it would be improving vehicles in Battlefield 6, using the Battlefield Labs platform as a testing ground for the biggest changes. Today, the team confirmed exactly what those incoming changes look like.

Chris Matte wrote on Steam:

Over the past months, we’ve been reviewing your feedback on vehicle performance across Battlefield Labs and the live game. While vehicles are broadly balanced from a statistical perspective, your feedback has made it clear that the moment-to-moment experience hasn’t always felt as satisfying as it should, particularly when it comes to how vehicle strengths and drawbacks play out in combat.

With that being said, vehicular combat is being made more predictable, making hits from anti-tank weapons more consistent and less dependent on angles and hit location. The damage ratings will ‘fall into a smaller set of clearly defined outcomes’ as opposed to a more complex model.

Additionally, vehicle health pools and damage modifiers have been examined and rebalanced to make things more realistic on a per-vehicle basis. Further, guided weapons will deal more consistent levels of damage against whatever they make contact with.

Elsewhere, vehicle survivability has been scrutinized, with DICE amending how repair mechanisms work, making these functions lose their efficacy over time. Health pools will take longer to start regenerating out of combat, but once the action kicks in, they’ll regain faster, and even critical damage won’t be enough to fully halt a vehicle’s regeneration factor.

Finally, control and responsiveness have been addressed, including turret rotation speed and base acceleration values. Those who prefer to sit in the sky will have new UI indicators that help them better identify incoming threats.

Matte highlighted:

Our goal is to give players the tools and confidence to take calculated risks, push objectives, and play a more active role on the battlefield when the situation calls for it, whether you’re on foot or pushing a control point in a tank.

Do you think these changes sound good? Let us know your thoughts on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that Metro 2039 has secured one million wishlists

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: PAC-MAN: Snack Breaks Releases Next Month
item.content:encoded:
A new animated series, PAC-MAN: Snack Breaks, has been revealed and will premiere on May 22, 2026.

PAC-MAN is one of the OGs of gaming, which close to all gamers must’ve heard about. Players have seen several PAC-MAN adaptations over the years, from the 1980’s cartoon to the 2023 movie PAC-MAN and the Ghost Adventures, and the latest reimagining for Amazon Prime’s Secret Level. Now, a new adaptation has arrived, and it will be featured on YouTube.

PAC-MAN Snack Breaks Teaser Trailer Revealed

A new teaser trailer has arrived on the PAC-MAN official YouTube channel, announcing the premiere of a new animated series, PAC-MAN: Snack Breaks, which will run on YouTube from May 22. The trailer features PAC-MAN himself moving out of a village filled with other PAC-MEN, as he progresses to his next adventure. He stops for a ‘Snack Break’ before we are teased with a vibrant, futuristic, maze-themed city.

Bandai Namco Entertainment America Inc. and Cartuna Productions have produced PAC-MAN Snack Breaks. The Old Guitarist’s Mike Cefalo will play PAC-MAN, and viewers will receive a new episode every month. Check out the description for the upcoming PAC-MAN animated series:

What adventures are being cooked up? What a-MAZE-ing challenges will PAC-MAN chomp through? Find out in PAC-MAN: Snack Breaks, where snack-sized moments turn into feasts of fun and life is not just a 1-player game. Join PAC-MAN along with a colorful cast of characters, some of whom will help PAC-MAN conquer his day-to-day quests, while others will serve up chaos on a whole new level.

In other news, a bidding war has ensued for the Battlefield video game adaptation, with five top-tier studios vying for the film rights. Additionally, the first trailer for Zach Cregger’s Resident Evil movie has finally been revealed to the general public. What are your thoughts on PAC-MAN Snack Breaks releasing next month? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Forza Horizon 6’s multiplayer modes. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.
